channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rivers State Government Moves to Resolve Bille Gas Leakage and Upgrade Local Health Facility
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Sir Siminalayi Fubara, don call for quick and full resolution of the gas leakage wey happen for Bille community inside Degema Local Government Area of the state. The governor al...

item.title: Severe June heatwave in Western Europe linked to 3,700 excess deaths across France, Belgium, and the Netherlands
item.description: A severe heatwave that affected parts of Western Europe in late June 2026 has been linked to at least 3,700 excess deaths across France, Belgium, and the Netherlands, according to preliminary governme...

item.title: Mass Celebrations After Mexico–Ecuador World Cup Match Registered Seismic Vibrations in Mexico City
item.description: Following Mexico’s 2026 World Cup round-of-32 victory over Ecuador, seismic monitoring systems in Mexico City detected unusual ground vibrations linked to fan celebrations. The match saw goals from Ju...

item.title: Critics praise ‘Silo’ season three as a standout return for Apple TV’s dystopian sci-fi series
item.description: The third season of Apple TV’s dystopian science fiction series ‘Silo’ has been met with strong critical acclaim following the release of its opening episode on 3 July. Early reviews describe the new ...

item.title: Tanzania–US sign five-year USD 3.1 billion health cooperation agreement to improve healthcare system
item.description: Tanzania and the United States have signed a five-year health cooperation Memorandum of Understanding valued at USD 3.1 billion aimed at strengthening and modernising Tanzania’s healthcare system. The...

item.title: Maria Landrock: A Legacy of German Cinema and Voice Acting
item.description: Maria Johanna Elisabeth Landrock (1923–1992) was a prominent German film and television actress, best known for her leading roles in Ufa films during the early 1940s. Born in Köpenick, Berlin, she tra...

item.title: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar meets predecessor Siddaramaiah marking one month in office
item.description: Karnataka Chief Minister DK Shivakumar met his predecessor and senior Congress leader Siddaramaiah at his official residence, Cauvery, in Bengaluru, as he completed one month in office. The meeting wa...

item.title: David Maraga signals possible alignment with Linda Mwananchi ahead of Gusii political rallies
item.description: Former Chief Justice David Maraga has indicated that he may be politically aligning himself with the Linda Mwananchi movement as the group prepares for upcoming rallies in Kisii and Nyamira counties. ...

item.title: Atiku demands independent inquiry into PFIPC scandal, urges Tinubu to act
item.description: Presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has demanded an immediate, transparent, and independent investigation into the PFIPC scandal, warning President Bola Ti...

item.title: Quebec couple wins $40 million Lotto Max jackpot after fishing trip
item.description: A Quebec couple in their 40s experienced a life-changing moment after discovering they had won a $40 million Lotto Max jackpot following a fishing trip. Mylène Bourgeois and Joseph Gosselin-Rompre, wh...

item.title: How U.S. Tech Companies and Government Narratives Shape the AI Competition with China
item.description: The article presents an analysis arguing that the global race for artificial intelligence is being shaped not only by technological competition but also by political and economic interests. Journalist...

item.title: Dozens killed as passenger bus falls into ravine in Zhob due to suspected brake failure
item.description: At least 40 people lost their lives and eight others were injured when a passenger bus plunged into a deep ravine in the Danasar area of Zhob, Balochistan. According to police and local officials, the...

item.title: Brent crude falls towards $70 amid easing Middle East tensions and inflation relief expectations
item.description: Global oil markets have continued to ease, with Brent crude slipping towards the $70 per barrel mark as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East show signs of easing. Diplomatic progress between the U...

item.title: Profile of late Tanimola linked to alleged phantom council appointment scandal
item.description: Dolapo Babatunde Tanimola, now deceased, has emerged as a central but mysterious figure in an alleged “phantom council” scandal being investigated by Nigerian authorities. According to reports referen...

item.title: Pasay City implements road closures and tight security ahead of Vice President Sara Duterte impeachment trial
item.description: Pasay City authorities have announced significant traffic rerouting and security preparations in anticipation of the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, which is set to begin at the Sena...

item.title: Pegasus spyware used to target EU lawmaker involved in investigation into surveillance abuses
item.description: Security researchers have confirmed that a European politician involved in investigating spyware misuse was himself targeted and hacked using NSO Group’s Pegasus spyware. The case involves Stelios Kou...

item.title: Kenya Kwanza leaders rally in Eldoret to promote government achievements and 2027 re-election bid
item.description: Senior Kenya Kwanza leaders, led by National Assembly Speaker Moses Wetang'ula and Kikuyu MP Kimani Ichung'wah, held a major political rally in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, where they rallied support ...

item.title: Profile of Joey Heatherton, American Actress and Entertainer of the 1960s and 1970s
item.description: The article provides a brief retrospective on Joey Heatherton, an American actress, singer, and dancer who rose to prominence during the 1960s and 1970s. Born in 1944, Heatherton became widely recogni...

item.title: Passenger bus crash near Balochistan-KP border leaves 40 dead and 8 injured
item.description: A tragic road accident took place when a Peshawar-bound passenger bus fell into a deep ravine shortly after leaving the Dhanasar area of Sherani district in Balochistan and crossing into Dera Ismail K...

item.title: BAFM Forum 2026 Day 1: Africa Advances Capital Market Integration, Youth Investment Push, and Economic Growth Updates
